ocr.text: The following analysis provides insight into Prologis Inc.'s operational performance, particularly focusing on Same-property
Net Operating Income (NOI) growth and lease expiry schedules. In 2023, the overall Same-property NOI growth was reported
at 5.2%, reflecting a robust increase in rental income across the portfolio. This growth trajectory is expected to enhance the
company’s financial resilience, with projections for 2024 indicating a further increase to 6.0%.
PropertyType  Same-PropertyMetrics
Period  5-YearCAGR(%)  CurrentValue
Industrial  NOIGrowth  Revenue(USD)  NetIncome(USD)  CAPEX(USD)
Logistics  7.5%  $400M  $250M  $40M  [HIDDEN]  4.8%  $450M
The upcoming lease expirations are notable, with approximately 1,500,000 square feet of space set to renew, positioning the
company to capitalize on favorable market conditions. Notably, the average rental rate is anticipated to remain stable at $12.00
per square foot, supporting the overall growth strategy.

PrologisIncPortfolio  SquareFootage  LeaseRate  OccupancyRate  RenewalRate  AverageTerm  MarketValue
AverageperYear  1,500,000  $12.80  91%  77%  4.8years  $18.6M  ProjectedGrowth  3%  5%  4%  2%  3.5%  4.5%
MarketOutlook  Stable  [HIDDEN]  Stable  Positive  Stable  Positive  RiskLevel  Low  Medium  Medium  Low  Medium  OverallAssessment
The lease expiry schedule indicates that 85% of the leases will be on a five-year term, ensuring a steady income stream.
Furthermore, the company anticipates additional capital from lease renewals, with expected contributions of $300M and $50M
in various segments. In this context, Prologis Inc.’s proactive management strategies and strong market positioning are expected
to yield an impressive $18M in growth, further solidifying its leadership in the logistics real estate sector. As the market outlook
remains positive, stakeholders can expect continued performance improvements moving forward.
